title: Start dates and clinical event dates cannot be in the future
description: Every *STDAT field and every clinical-event date (visit dates, treatment dates, sample-collection dates) gets constraint `. <= today()` and constraint_message 'Date cannot be in the future.' Exceptions: scheduled or planned dates (e.g. next-visit-planned-date) which deliberately reference a future moment.
rationale: Clinical-event dates by definition record something that has happened. A future date in such a field is either a data-entry typo or a misunderstanding of what the field captures. Scheduled/planned dates are explicitly distinct fields where future values are legitimate and the constraint should NOT fire.

ensure.field.constraint_message: Date cannot be in the future.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} pinned with `. <= today()` constraint and standard message. Verify this is NOT a scheduled/planned-date field (e.g. NEXT_VISIT_DAT, PLANNED_*) where future values are legitimate; if so, this convention should be overridden at study scope.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7B during Phase B.1c-final. Overlap candidate with B.0 inventory row §2 (general future-date constraint) — to be resolved when System 2 lands in Phase B.1d. Hybrid because the scheduled/planned-date exception requires judgment per field; the structural ensure handles the common case, the flag surfaces the field for human review. Field-name allowlist for clinical event dates is illustrative and incomplete; study-scope overrides should add domain-specific names.
